You can visit the ZenвҖҷin House from JUJUTSU KAISEN. Just make sure you get screenshots of the anime before you go or you will have to rely on your memory like I did.  The Edo Tokyo Open Air Museum is super cool to visit and actually has a building that inspired the spot sprite scene from SPIRITED AWAY. ItвҖҷs a bit of a journey but I recommend checking. ESPECIALLY if youвҖҷre there during cherry blossom season.  #makizenin #jjk #jujutsukaisen #animepilgrimage #naoyazenin

Did you know the Shibuya Incident actually happened here? If you've watched Jujutsu Kaisen, walking through Shibuya at night feels entirely different. The scramble crossing where the curtain fell. The underground B5F concourse where Gojo fought. The neon-lit streets of Dogenzaka. Every single location exists in real life, and standing there makes you feel like you've stepped right into the anime. We've mapped out the exact spots you need to visit to recreate the experience. It takes about 2 hours, it's completely free, and it's an absolute must-do for any JJK fan visiting Tokyo. Jujutsu Kaisen is a dark fantasy anime set in a world where negative human emotions create cursed spirits that terrorize society. The story follows Yuji Itadori, a physically gifted high school student who accidentally becomes the host of Ryomen Sukuna, one of the most powerful and evil curses in history, after swallowing a cursed object to save his friends. Instead of being executed immediately, Yuji is taken in by the Tokyo Jujutsu High and trained as a jujutsu sorcerer under the guidance of the laid-back but deadly Satoru Gojo. The anime blends intense action, horror, and philosophy as sorcerers fight curses, explore the cost of power, and question what it truly means to live a meaningful life in a cruel, unforgiving world. #jjk #anime #fyp

Part 26 | Jujutsu Kaisen Real Life Location in Japan рҹҮҜрҹҮө (Kabukiza Theatre, Tokyo) рҹ“Қ Description A real-life location from the anime вҖңJujutsu Kaisen вҖ“ Culling Game Arc.вҖқ This video features Kabukiza Theatre in Tokyo, Japan. The atmosphere around this area strongly resembles scenes from the anime. Kabukiza is one of the most famous traditional theaters in Japan and a well-known landmark in Ginza, Tokyo. Many anime fans visit places like this for Seichi Junrei (Anime Pilgrimage). If youвҖҷre a Jujutsu Kaisen fan, this is a place you might want to visit someday! #JujutsuKaisen #е‘ӘиЎ“е»»жҲҰ #иҒ–ең°е·ЎзӨј #anime #animejapan

вҖӢрҹҡЁ Calling all Jujutsu Kaisen fans! рҹҡЁ вҖӢNewly minted JJK manholes just dropped in Sendai (March 24th) to commemorate the epic Sendai Colony fights, and of course, I had to go hunting for them! рҹҸҜвңЁ вҖӢIf you're planning an anime pilgrimage, here is your quick guide to tracking them down: вҖӢрҹ“Қ Izumi-Chuo Station: You can easily spot 3 of these beautifully detailed covers right around the station area. вҖӢрҹ“Қ Yurtec Stadium: Just an 8-minute walk from the station, you'll find the 4th one located near the park. вҖӢвҡ пёҸ My personal pro-tip: I went at night and the stadium area was totally deserted. I wasn't about to wander into a dark park alone to find the final manhole (I'm not trying to test my cursed energy! рҹ‘»), so I highly recommend doing this hunt during the day! вҳҖпёҸ вҖӢрҹ“Ң Save this guide for your next visit to Sendai! вҖӢ#JujutsuKaisen #JJK #Sendai #AnimePilgrimage #yutaokkotsu
